deficit_span.speaker: teacher
deficit_span.verbatim_quote: student i as soon as you find a little mistake you often give up and think you can t do it but if you look at it for 10 seconds you would have found that mistake right

context_excerpt.speaker: student
context_excerpt.text: i didn t get the answer

context_excerpt.speaker: teacher
context_excerpt.text: 8 minus 1 doesn t equal 8 does it student i as soon as you find a little mistake you often give up and think you can t do it but if you look at it for 10 seconds you would have found that mistake right turn around right so if you find a discrepancy again a difference i want you to take some time and figure it out on your own okay it was the other way you didn t even solve it the other way yet if you re doing the standard algorithm i need to see you doing it correctly you didn t show any work on it please use a different strategy if you re not sure of it we ll do it later on in the week okay do you guys agree no

context_excerpt.speaker: teacher
context_excerpt.text: did you check the other three and did you guys all get the same answers or did you disagree on something

scenes.rationale: The teacher attributes a habitual motivational failing to the student ('you often give up and think you can't do it'), characterizing the student's disposition rather than describing the specific error or task, which is a deficit attribution for behavior/motivation.
